subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF, OECD, WJP, RSF, TI) + NZ official (Stats NZ, RBNZ, Te Whatu Ora) + NZ independent press (RNZ, NZ Herald, Newsroom).
Source-lean: HIGH Anglophone (NZ is natively English-language); cross-checked via multilateral indices rather than press tone.
Standout drags: youth emigration / ageing demographics and seismic-plus-climate disaster exposure.

economic.notes: A credible, pioneering inflation-targeting central bank and open trade, against a recent recession and high household/housing debt.

fiscal_state.notes: Moderate debt with a recent primary deficit (~2.4% GDP) and slow consolidation; 2024 contraction rebounding to ~2-3% growth. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: The RBNZ is a genuinely independent inflation-targeting pioneer, cutting on its own read with no political pressure. (RBNZ)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The NZD is a free-floating own currency but not a reserve currency; neutral. (RBNZ)

inflation.notes: Back in the 1-3% band (~3.1% end-2025) with a near-term oil-driven spike forecast before re-settling. (RBNZ)

banking_stability.notes: Four Australian-owned majors (~84% of lending), well-capitalised and stress-test-resilient; high household/housing-debt concentration the offset. (RBNZ FSR)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Stable open-trade policy and strong agri/dairy exports, no resource curse; heavy China-market concentration the main risk. (IMF)

institutional.notes: World-class rule of law and statistics; press freedom threatened by a collapsing commercial-media market, not the state.

rule_of_law.notes: Among the strongest (WJP 5/143); an independent judiciary; a minor erosion narrative but world-class. (WJP 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: Stats NZ is independent and credible, with no interference. (Stats NZ)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A capable Westminster bureaucracy, but coalition-era spending restraint and public-sector cuts strain delivery. (RNZ)

press_freedom.notes: RSF 16/180 (2025), strong, but structurally threatened by a collapsing commercial media market (closures, layoffs). (RSF)

political_social.notes: A stable MMP democracy with a lawful, selective migration system; the long-term drag is youth emigration and ageing.

political_stability.notes: A National-led coalition stable since 2023; a peaceful, functional MMP democracy. (RNZ)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong protections and a robust right to protest (a large Treaty hikoi proceeded freely). (WJP)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A lawful, durable, selective skilled-migrant system; the April 2025 Active Investor Plus relaxation widened openness for the wealthy. Open but tiered. (Immigration NZ)

social_cohesion.notes: The Treaty Principles Bill was defeated (Apr 2025) after record submissions/protests - institutions absorbed the strain, but Crown-Maori tension and immigration debate persist. (Justice Committee)

demographics.notes: Ageing plus record citizen departures (mostly young, to Australia) with net migration collapsing. (Stats NZ; OECD)

geopolitical.notes: Geographically isolated and peaceful; a Five Eyes member hedging the US-China relationship.

alliance_reliability.notes: A Five Eyes member tilting toward the US/AUKUS Pillar 2 while keeping the popular nuclear-free policy; reliable but hedging China. (Chatham House)

conflict_involvement.notes: Geographically isolated, no active conflicts (Global Peace Index 3rd). (GPI)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Follows Western sanctions, not a target; no capital controls; a free-floating NZD. (RBNZ)

physical_practical.notes: Very low crime, against a strained public-health system, an infrastructure deficit, and seismic/storm exposure.

climate.notes: High seismic, flood, and ex-tropical-cyclone exposure (2023 Cyclone Gabrielle ~NZ$14.5bn), rising in frequency. (academic; insurance)

healthcare.notes: A universal system under acute strain: a large planned-care waitlist and some declined referrals; health spend below peers. (Te Whatu Ora; OAG)

infrastructure.notes: An acknowledged infrastructure deficit (hospital beds/theatres as constraints); adequate but underinvested. (Te Whatu Ora)

crime_safety.notes: Very low violent crime, with homicide roughly halved and ram-raids sharply down (GPI 3rd). (NZ Police)

disaster_insurance.notes: A functional Natural Hazards Commission with record reinsurance, but rising premiums, flood-zone insurance retreat, and an unresolved managed-retreat funding debate. (Natural Hazards Commission)

summary: A stable, peaceful MMP democracy with world-class rule of law, a credible pioneering central bank, very low crime, and a lawful (if selective) migration system. The drags are a recent recession with high household/housing debt, youth emigration and ageing eroding the long-term population base, a strained public-health system and infrastructure deficit, and seismic-plus-climate disaster exposure.
